chief_complaint: A 55-year-old male presents with concerns about his cholesterol management.
history: The patient has a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia, and is a current smoker. He has a family history of premature cardiovascular disease. He is currently taking atorvastatin 20mg daily.
exam: Vital signs are within normal limits. Physical examination is unremarkable.
labs: Low-density lipoprotein cholesterol level is 190 mg/dL or greater.
